00026 /*=========================================================================
00027  * SYSTEM:    Verifier
00028  * SUBSYSTEM: class loader.
00029  * FILE:      classloader.c
00030  * OVERVIEW:  Routines for loading and resolving class definitions.
00031  *            These routines should not be depending upon the interpreter
00032  *            or the garbage collector.
00033  *=======================================================================*/

00050  * Globals and extern declarations

00055 extern ClassClass *allocClassClass();

00067 /*=========================================================================
00068  * FUNCTION:      AddBinClass
00069  * OVERVIEW:      Used by createInternalClass1() and createFakeArrayClass()
00070  *                to add a class in the class table.
00071  * INTERFACE:
00072  *   parameters:  ClassClass: cb
00073  *
00074  *   returns:     nothing
00075  *=======================================================================*/
00076 void
00077 AddBinClass(ClassClass * cb)
00078 {
00079     register int left, right, middle, result, i;
00080     char *name = cbName(cb);
00081     struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *loader = cbLoader(cb);
00082 
00083     BINCLASS_LOCK();
00084     left = 0;
00085     right = nbinclasses - 1;
00086     result = 1;
00087     while (left <= right) {
00088         ClassClass *cb1;
00089         middle = (left+right)/2;
00090         cb1 = binclasses[middle];
00091         result = strcmp(name, cbName(cb1));
00092         if (result == 0) {
00093             if (loader < cbLoader(cb1)) {
00094                 result = -1;
00095             } else if (loader > cbLoader(cb1)) {
00096                 result = 1;
00097             } else {
00098                 result = 0;
00099             }
00100         }
00101         if (result < 0) {
00102             right = middle-1;
00103         } else if (result > 0) {
00104             left = middle+1;
00105         } else {
00106             break;
00107         }
00108     }
00109     if (result != 0) {
00110         if (nbinclasses >= sizebinclasses) {
00111             if (binclasses == 0)
00112                 binclasses = (ClassClass **)
00113                 sysMalloc(sizeof(ClassClass *) * (sizebinclasses = 50));
00114             else
00115                 binclasses = (ClassClass **)
00116                 sysRealloc(binclasses, sizeof(ClassClass *)
00117                     * (sizebinclasses = nbinclasses * 2));
00118         }
00119         if (binclasses == 0)
00120             goto unlock;
00121         right++;
00122         for (i = nbinclasses; i > right; i--) {
00123             binclasses[i] = binclasses[i-1];
00124         }
00125         binclasses[right] = cb;
00126         nbinclasses++;
00127     }
00128 
00129 unlock:
00130     BINCLASS_UNLOCK();
00131 }
00132 
00133 /*=========================================================================
00134  * FUNCTION:      DelBinClass
00135  * OVERVIEW:      Intended for allowing deletion of classes from the class
00136  *                table.
00137  *
00138  * INTERFACE:
00139  *   parameters:  ClassClass: cb
00140  *
00141  *   returns:     nothing
00142  *=======================================================================*/
00143 void
00144 DelBinClass(ClassClass * cb)
00145 {
00146     register int i, j;
00147     BINCLASS_LOCK();
00148     for (i = nbinclasses; --i >= 0; )
00149         if (binclasses[i] == cb) {
00150             nbinclasses--;
00151             for (j = i; j < nbinclasses; j++) {
00152                 binclasses[j] = binclasses[j+1];
00153             }
00154             break;
00155         }
00156     BINCLASS_UNLOCK();
00157 }
00158 
00159 /*=========================================================================
00160  * FUNCTION:      MakeClassSticky
00161  * OVERVIEW:      Used to lock certain system classes into memory during
00162  *                initialization.
00163  *
00164  * INTERFACE:
00165  *   parameters:  ClassClass: cb
00166  *
00167  *   returns:     nothing
00168  *=======================================================================*/
00169 void
00170 MakeClassSticky(ClassClass *cb)
00171 {
00172     /* monitorEnter(obj_monitor(cb));   */
00173     CCSet(cb, Sticky);
00174     /* monitorExit(obj_monitor(cb));    */
00175 }
00176 
00177 /*=========================================================================
00178  * FUNCTION:      LoadClassFromFile
00179  * OVERVIEW:      Loads a .class file normally from disk or a null if it fails.
00180  *                When the interpreter requests for a file, it is actually
00181  *                looking for a classblock structure to be created, and the
00182  *                only way it can get one of those is by loading a compiled
00183  *                class.
00184  *                OpenCode() tries to open a .class file first. If it fails
00185  *                to open the file, it returns a non-zero status. If it
00186  *                returns a valid file descriptor, this usually means that
00187  *                this is a valid .class file.
00188  *                It then invokes createInternalClass() to actually create the
00189  *                internal representation of the class.
00190  *
00191  * INTERFACE:
00192  *   parameters:  char*: file name
00193  *                char*: directory
00194  *                char*: class name
00195  *
00196  *   returns:     nothing
00197  *=======================================================================*/
00198 static ClassClass *
00199 LoadClassFromFile(char *fn, char *dir, char *class_name)
00200 {
00201     extern int OpenCode(char *, char *, char *, struct stat*);
00202     struct stat st;
00203     ClassClass *cb = 0;
00204     int codefd = -1;
00205     unsigned char *external_class;
00206     char *detail;
00207 
00208     codefd = OpenCode(fn, NULL, dir, &st);
00209 
00210     if (codefd < 0)     /* open failed */
00211         return 0;
00212 
00213     /* Snarf the file into memory. */
00214     external_class = (unsigned char *)sysMalloc(st.st_size);
00215     if (external_class == 0)
00216         goto failed;
00217     if (sysRead(codefd, external_class, st.st_size) != st.st_size)
00218         goto failed;
00219     sysClose(codefd);
00220     codefd = -1;
00221 
00222     /* Create the internal class */
00223     cb = allocClassClass();
00224     if (cb == NULL ||
00225         !createInternalClass(external_class, external_class + st.st_size,
00226                  cb, NULL, class_name, &detail)) {
00227         sysFree(external_class);
00228         goto failed;
00229     }
00230     sysFree(external_class);
00231 
00232     if (verbose)
00233         jio_fprintf(stderr, "[Loaded %s]\n", fn);
00234 
00235     return cb;
00236 failed:
00237     if (codefd >= 0)
00238         sysClose(codefd);
00239     if (cb != 0)
00240         FreeClass(cb);
00241     return 0;
00242 }
00243 
00244 /*=========================================================================
00245  * FUNCTION:      LoadClassFromZip
00246  * TYPE:          load class from a JAR or Zip file
00247  * OVERVIEW:      Called by LoadClassLocally for loading classes from a Zip.
00248  *
00249  *                This function loads a .class file normally from a Zip or
00250  *                JAR file.
00251  *                It returns the class when it succeeds or NULL if it fails.
00252  *
00253  * INTERFACE:
00254  *   parameters:  zip_t: zip file entry
00255  *                char*: class file name to search for loading
00256  *   returns:     Pointer to ClassClass when it succeeds, or NULL if it fails.
00257  *=======================================================================*/
00258 static ClassClass *
00259 LoadClassFromZip(zip_t *zipEntry, char *class_name)
00260 {
00261     ClassClass *cb = 0;
00262     JAR_DataStreamPtr jdstream = NULL;
00263     unsigned char *external_class;
00264     int data_length;
00265     char *detail;
00266 
00267     jdstream = loadJARfile (zipEntry, class_name);
00268 
00269     if (jdstream == NULL)
00270         goto failed;
00271 
00272     external_class = jdstream->data;
00273     data_length = jdstream->dataLen;
00274 
00275     /* Create the internal class */
00276     cb = allocClassClass();
00277     if (cb == NULL ||
00278         !createInternalClass(external_class, external_class + data_length,
00279                  cb, NULL, class_name, &detail)) {
00280         goto failed;
00281     }
00282     if (jdstream != NULL) {
00283         freeBytes(jdstream);
00284     }
00285 
00286     if (verbose) {
00287         jio_fprintf(stderr, "[Loaded %s] from [%s]\n", class_name,
00288                                                        zipEntry->name);
00289     }
00290     return cb;
00291 failed:
00292     if (cb != 0)
00293         FreeClass(cb);
00294     if (jdstream != NULL) {
00295         freeBytes(jdstream);
00296     }
00297     return 0;
00298 }
00299 
00300 /*=========================================================================
00301  * FUNCTION:      LoadClassLocally
00302  * OVERVIEW:      Find a class file that is somewhere local, and not from a
00303  *                class loader.
00304  *                It still needs to be searched using the classpath.
00305  *
00306  * INTERFACE:
00307  *   parameters:  char* : class file name
00308  *   returns:     Pointer to ClassClass when it succeeds, or NULL if it fails.
00309  *=======================================================================*/
00310 ClassClass *LoadClassLocally(char *name)
00311 {
00312     ClassClass *cb = 0;
00313     cpe_t **cpp;
00314 
00315     if (name[0] == DIR_SEPARATOR || name[0] == SIGNATURE_ARRAY)
00316         return 0;
00317 
00318     for (cpp = sysGetClassPath(); cpp && *cpp != 0; cpp++) {
00319         cpe_t *cpe = *cpp;
00320         char *path;
00321 
00322         if (cpe->type == CPE_DIR) {
00323             path = (char *)sysMalloc(strlen(cpe->u.dir)
00324                 + sizeof(LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR)
00325                 + strlen(name)
00326                 + strlen(JAVAOBJEXT)
00327                 + 2);  /* 2 is for the . and the \0 */
00328             if (sprintf(path,
00329                  "%s%c%s." JAVAOBJEXT, cpe->u.dir,
00330                  LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR, name) == -1) {
00331                 sysFree(path);
00332                 return 0;
00333             }
00334             if ((cb = LoadClassFromFile(sysNativePath(path),
00335                                         cpe->u.dir, name))) {
00336                 sysFree(path);
00337                 return cb;
00338             }
00339         } else if (cpe->type == CPE_ZIP) {
00340             if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00341                 jio_fprintf(stderr, "Loading classes from a ZIP file... \n");
00342             if ((cb = LoadClassFromZip(cpe->u.zip, name))) {
00343                 return cb;
00344             }
00345         }
00346 
00347     }
00348     return cb;
00349 }
00350 
00351 /*=========================================================================
00352  * FUNCTION:      stat_source
00353  * OVERVIEW:      Unused by the Verifier, but it is too late to remove it now.
00354  *
00355  * INTERFACE:
00356  *   parameters:  ClassClass *: cb
00357  *                struct stat*: s
00358  *                char*: pathbuf
00359  *                int: maxlen
00360  *   returns:     char *, or NULL if it fails.
00361  *=======================================================================*/
00362 char *
00363 stat_source(ClassClass *cb, struct stat *s, char *pathbuf, int maxlen)
00364 {
00365 #define NAMEBUFLEN 255
00366     char nm[NAMEBUFLEN];
00367     char *p, *q, *lp;
00368     cpe_t **cpp;
00369 
00370     /* don't bother searching if absolute */
00371     /* IMPL_NOTE: only here for compatibility */
00372     if (sysIsAbsolute(cbSourceName(cb))) {
00373         if (sysStat(cbSourceName(cb), s) == 0) {
00374             if (jio_snprintf(pathbuf, maxlen, "%s", cbSourceName(cb)) == -1) {
00375                 return 0;
00376             }
00377             return pathbuf;
00378         } else {
00379             return 0;
00380         }
00381     }
00382 
00383     /* parse the package name */
00384     p = cbName(cb);
00385     if (strlen(p) > NAMEBUFLEN - 1) {
00386         return 0;
00387     }
00388     for (q = lp = nm ; *p ; p++) {
00389         if (*p == DIR_SEPARATOR) {
00390             *q++ = LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR;
00391             lp = q;
00392         } else {
00393             *q++ = *p;
00394         }
00395     }
00396 
00397     /* append the source file name */
00398     p = cbSourceName(cb);
00399     if (strlen(p) + (lp - nm) > NAMEBUFLEN - 1) {
00400         return 0;
00401     }
00402     for (; *p ; p++) {
00403         *lp++ = (*p == DIR_SEPARATOR ? LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR : *p);
00404     }
00405     *lp = '\0';
00406 
00407     /* search the class path */
00408     for (cpp = sysGetClassPath() ; cpp && *cpp != 0 ; cpp++) {
00409         cpe_t *cpe = *cpp;
00410         if (cpe->type == CPE_DIR) {
00411             if (jio_snprintf(pathbuf, maxlen, "%s%c%s",
00412                              cpe->u.dir, LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR, nm) == -1) {
00413                 return 0;
00414             }
00415             if (sysStat(pathbuf, s) == 0) {
00416                 return pathbuf;
00417             }
00418         }
00419     }
00420     return 0;
00421 }
00422 
00423 /*=========================================================================
00424  * Globals and externs for Internal Class representation
00425  *=======================================================================*/
00426 struct CICmallocs {
00427     struct CICmallocs *next;
00428     void * alignment_padding;
00429 
00430 /* Whatever follows will be 8-byte aligned, if the structure itself is
00431    8-byte aligned. */
00432 };
00433 
00434 typedef struct CICmallocs CICmallocs;
00435 
00436 struct CICcontext {
00437     unsigned char *ptr;
00438     unsigned char *end_ptr;
00439     ClassClass *cb;
00440     jmp_buf jump_buffer;
00441     char **detail;
00442 
00443     int pass;         /* two passes, 1 or 2 */
00444     int malloc_size;  /* space needed for everything other than <clinit> */
00445     int clinit_size;  /* space needed for the <clinit> method */
00446     int in_clinit;    /* indicates whether we are loading <clinit> method */
00447 
00448     struct {
00449         CICmallocs *mallocs; /* list of memory blocks used in the first pass */
00450 
00451         void * alignment_padding;
00452                              /* Whatever follows will be 8-byte aligned */
00453     } pass1;
00454 
00455     struct {
00456         char *malloc_buffer; /* used to hold everything other than <clinit> */
00457         char *malloc_ptr;    /* current point of allocation */
00458 
00459         char *clinit_buffer; /* used to hold the <clinit> method */
00460         char *clinit_ptr;    /* current point of allocation */
00461     } pass2;
00462 
00463 };
00464 
00465 typedef struct CICcontext CICcontext;
00466 
00467 static char *getAsciz(CICcontext *, bool);
00468 static char *getAscizFromClass(CICcontext *, int i);
00469 
00470 static uint8_t get1byte(CICcontext *);
00471 static uint16_t get2bytes(CICcontext *);
00472 static uint32_t get4bytes(CICcontext *);
00473 static void getNbytes(CICcontext *, int count, char *buffer);
00474 static void *allocNBytes(CICcontext *, int size);
00475 static void freeBuffers(CICcontext *);
00476 
00477 static void LoadConstantPool(CICcontext *);
00478 static void verifyUTF8String(CICcontext *context, char* bytes,
00479                              unsigned short length);
00480 
00481 static void ReadInCode(CICcontext *, struct methodblock *);
00482 static void ReadLineTable(CICcontext *, struct methodblock *mb);
00483 static void ReadExceptions(CICcontext *, struct methodblock *);
00484 static void ReadLocalVars(CICcontext *, struct methodblock *mb);
00485 
00486 static void
00487 createInternalClass0(CICcontext *context, ClassClass *cb,
00488              struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *loader, char *name);
00489 
00490 bool
00491 createInternalClass1(unsigned char *ptr, unsigned char *end_ptr,
00492              ClassClass *cb, struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *loader,
00493              char *name, char **detail);
00494 
00495 /*=========================================================================
00496  * FUNCTION:      createInternalClass
00497  * OVERVIEW:      Invoked by LoadClassFromFile() or LoadClassFromZip() to
00498  *                create an internal class. See createInternalClass1() for
00499  *                details.
00500  *
00501  * INTERFACE:
00502  *   parameters:  unsigned char *: ptr
00503  *                unsigned char *: end_ptr
00504  *                ClassClass *: cb
00505  *                struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *: loader
00506  *                char *: name
00507  *                char **: detail
00508  *
00509  *   returns:     bool_t
00510  *=======================================================================*/
00511 bool
00512 createInternalClass(unsigned char *ptr, unsigned char *end_ptr,
00513             ClassClass *cb, struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *loader,
00514             char *name, char **detail)
00515 {
00516     bool res;
00517     res = createInternalClass1(ptr, end_ptr, cb, loader, name, detail);
00518 
00519     return res;
00520 }
00521 
00522 /*=========================================================================
00523  * FUNCTION:      JAVA_ERROR
00524  * OVERVIEW:      Verifier error processing function.
00525  *
00526  * INTERFACE:
00527  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: context
00528  *                char *      : name
00529  *
00530  *   returns:     nothing
00531  *=======================================================================*/
00532 void JAVA_ERROR(CICcontext *context, char *name) {
00533     printCurrentClassName();
00534     *(context->detail) = name;
00535     EE()->class_loading_msg = name;
00536     fprintf(stderr, "Class loading error: %s\n", name);
00537     exit(1);
00538 }
00539 
00540 /*=========================================================================
00541  * FUNCTION:      createInternalClass1
00542  * OVERVIEW:      Auxiliary function for creating an internal class.
00543  *                Invoked by createInternalClass().
00544  *
00545  *                Creates an internal class file from the indicated data.
00546  *                It should be in a buffer for which the first byte is at
00547  *                *ptr and the last byte is just before *end_ptr.
00548  *                The class's classloader is indicated by the classloader
00549  *                argument.
00550  *
00551  *                We go through the buffer twice. In the first pass, we
00552  *                determine the amount of storage needed by the class.
00553  *                We then allocate a single chunk of memory, free the
00554  *                temporary storage, and load from the buffer for the second
00555  *                time.
00556  *
00557  *                Since all storage needed by the class initialization method
00558  *                <clinit> can be freed after the class is loaded, we count
00559  *                the <clinit> space needs separately and store the <clinit>
00560  *                method in a separate chunk of memory.
00561  *
00562  * INTERFACE:
00563  *   parameters:  unsigned char *: ptr
00564  *                unsigned char *: end_ptr
00565  *                ClassClass *: cb
00566  *                struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *: loader
00567  *                char *: name
00568  *                char **: detail
00569  *
00570  *   returns:     bool_t
00571  *=======================================================================*/
00572 bool
00573 createInternalClass1(unsigned char *ptr, unsigned char *end_ptr,
00574              ClassClass *cb, struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *loader,
00575              char *name, char **detail)
00576 {
00577     struct CICcontext context_block;
00578     struct CICcontext *context = &context_block;
00579 
00580     /* Set up the context */
00581     context->ptr = ptr;
00582     context->end_ptr = end_ptr;
00583     context->cb = cb;
00584     context->detail = detail;
00585 
00586     /* initialize the remaining fields of the context block */
00587     context->pass = 0;
00588     context->malloc_size = 0;
00589     context->clinit_size = 0;
00590     context->in_clinit = 0;
00591     context->pass1.mallocs = 0;
00592 
00593     context->pass2.malloc_buffer = NULL;
00594     context->pass2.malloc_ptr = NULL;
00595     context->pass2.clinit_buffer = NULL;
00596     context->pass2.clinit_ptr = NULL;
00597 
00598     if (setjmp(context->jump_buffer)) {
00599 
00600         /* We've gotten an error of some sort
00601          * See comments below about zeroing these
00602          * two fields before freeing the temporary
00603          * buffer.
00604          */
00605 
00606         cbConstantPool(cb) = NULL;
00607         cbFields(cb) = NULL;
00608 
00609         /* Zero out the method so that freeClass will
00610          * not try to free the clinit method.
00611          */
00612 
00613         cbMethodsCount(cb) = 0;
00614         freeBuffers(context);
00615         return false;
00616     }
00617 
00618     /* The first pass allows us to uncover any class format
00619      * errors and find out the size of the buffer needed.
00620      */
00621 
00622     context->pass = 1;
00623     createInternalClass0(context, cb, loader, name);
00624 
00625     /* We must set the following two fields to zero before we free
00626      * the temporary buffers, because markClassClass may scan a
00627      * partially constructed class block in the second pass.
00628      * If these two fields are set to zero, markClassClass will
00629      * not scan the constant pool and field blocks, which may
00630      * point to freed memory.
00631      */
00632 
00633     cbConstantPool(cb) = NULL;
00634     cbFields(cb) = NULL;
00635 
00636     /* Zero out the method so that freeClass will not try
00637      * to free the clinit method.
00638      */
00639 
00640     cbMethodsCount(cb) = 0;
00641     freeBuffers(context);
00642 
00643     context->ptr = ptr;   /* rewind the raw class data */
00644 
00645     if (context->malloc_size > 0) {
00646         context->pass2.malloc_buffer =
00647                    (char *)sysCalloc(1, context->malloc_size);
00648 
00649         if (context->pass2.malloc_buffer == 0)
00650             JAVA_ERROR(context, "out of memory");
00651     }
00652 
00653     if (context->clinit_size > 0) {
00654         context->pass2.clinit_buffer =
00655                    (char *)sysCalloc(1, context->clinit_size * sizeof(char));
00656 
00657         if (context->pass2.clinit_buffer == 0) {
00658             sysFree(context->pass2.malloc_buffer);
00659             JAVA_ERROR(context, "out of memory");
00660         }
00661     }
00662 
00663     context->pass2.malloc_ptr = context->pass2.malloc_buffer;
00664     context->pass2.clinit_ptr = context->pass2.clinit_buffer;
00665 
00666     /* The second pass accomplishes the real task. */
00667 
00668     context->pass = 2;
00669     createInternalClass0(context, cb, loader, name);
00670 
00671     /* Valid class - let's put it in the class table. */
00672     AddBinClass(cb);
00673 
00674     return true;
00675 }
00676 
00677 /*=========================================================================
00678  * FUNCTION:      createInternalClass0
00679  * OVERVIEW:      Auxiliary function invoked by createInternalClass1() during
00680  *                the second pass to actually load the internal class file
00681  *                structures such as the constant pool, method blocks, field
00682  *                blocks and so on.
00683  *
00684  * INTERFACE:
00685  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: ptr
00686  *                ClassClass *: cb
00687  *                struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *: loader
00688  *                char *: name
00689  *
00690  *   returns:     nothing
00691  *=======================================================================*/
00692 static void
00693 createInternalClass0(CICcontext *context, ClassClass *cb,
00694              struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *loader, char *name)
00695 {
00696     int i, j, len;
00697     char buff[BUFSIZ];
00698     char *UTFname = &buff[0];
00699     union cp_item_type *constant_pool;
00700     unsigned char *type_table;
00701     int attribute_count;
00702     unsigned fields_count;
00703     struct methodblock *mb;
00704     struct fieldblock *fb;
00705     struct Classjava_lang_Class *ucb = unhand(cb);
00706     bool have_inner_classes;
00707 
00708     if (get4bytes(context) != JAVA_CLASSFILE_MAGIC)
00709         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Bad magic number");
00710 
00711     ucb->minor_version = get2bytes(context);
00712     ucb->major_version = get2bytes(context);
00713     ucb->loader = loader;
00714 
00715     /* Ignore version # so that the preverifier can work with JDK 1.4 onwards */
00716     /*
00717     if (ucb->major_version != JAVA_VERSION)
00718         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Bad major version number");
00719     */
00720 
00721     LoadConstantPool(context);
00722     constant_pool = ucb->constantpool;
00723     type_table = constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
00724 
00725     ucb->access = get2bytes(context) & ACC_WRITTEN_FLAGS;
00726 
00727     /* Get the name of the class */
00728     i = get2bytes(context); /* index in constant pool of class */
00729     ucb->name = getAscizFromClass(context, i);
00730 
00731     /* Conversion for Japanese filenames */
00732     len = native2utf8(name, UTFname, BUFSIZ);
00733 
00734     if (name != NULL && strcmp(ucb->name, UTFname) != 0)
00735         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Wrong name");
00736     constant_pool[i].clazz = cb;
00737     CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_SET_RESOLVED(type_table, i);
00738 
00739     if (loader) {
00740         /* We don't trust a classloader to do the right thing. . . */
00741         ClassClass **pcb, **end_pcb;
00742         char *name = ucb->name;
00743         if (name == NULL || !IsLegalClassname(name, false)) {
00744             JAVA_ERROR(context, "Bad name");
00745         }
00746         BINCLASS_LOCK();
00747         for (pcb = binclasses, end_pcb = pcb + nbinclasses;
00748                                pcb < end_pcb; pcb++) {
00749             ClassClass *cb = *pcb;
00750             if ((cbLoader(cb) == loader) && (strcmp(name, cbName(cb)) == 0))
00751                 break;
00752         }
00753         BINCLASS_UNLOCK();
00754         if (pcb < end_pcb)
00755             /* There's already a class with the same name and loader */
00756             JAVA_ERROR(context, "Duplicate name");
00757     }
00758 
00759     /* Get the super class name. */
00760     i = get2bytes(context); /* index in constant pool of class */
00761     if (i > 0) {
00762         ucb->super_name = getAscizFromClass(context, i);
00763         if (!IsLegalClassname(ucb->super_name, false)) {
00764             JAVA_ERROR(context, "Bad superclass name");
00765         }
00766     }
00767 
00768     i = ucb->implements_count = get2bytes(context);
00769     if (i > 0) {
00770         int j;
00771         ucb->implements = allocNBytes(context, i * sizeof(short));
00772         for (j = 0; j < i; j++) {
00773             ucb->implements[j] = get2bytes(context);
00774         }
00775     }
00776 
00777     fields_count = ucb->fields_count = get2bytes(context);
00778     if (fields_count > 0)
00779         ucb->fields = (struct fieldblock *)
00780           allocNBytes(context, ucb->fields_count * sizeof(struct fieldblock));
00781     for (i = fields_count, fb = ucb->fields; --i >= 0; fb++) {
00782         bool has_constant_value = false;
00783         fieldclass(fb) = cb;
00784         fb->access = get2bytes(context) & ACC_WRITTEN_FLAGS;
00785         fb->name = getAsciz(context, false);
00786         fb->signature = getAsciz(context, false);
00787         attribute_count = get2bytes(context);
00788         for (j = 0; j < (int)attribute_count; j++) {
00789             char *attr_name = getAsciz(context, false);
00790             int length = get4bytes(context);
00791             if (strcmp(attr_name, "ConstantValue") == 0) {
00792                 if (fb->access & ACC_STATIC) {
00793                     if (length != 2) {
00794                         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Wrong size for VALUE attribute");
00795                     }
00796                     if (has_constant_value) {
00797                         JAVA_ERROR(context,
00798                                    "Duplicate ConstantValue attribute");
00799                     }
00800                     has_constant_value = true;
00801                     fb->access |= ACC_VALKNOWN;
00802                     /* we'll change this below */
00803                     fb->u.offset = get2bytes(context);
00804                 } else {
00805                     getNbytes(context, length, NULL);
00806                 }
00807             } else if (strcmp(attr_name, "Deprecated") == 0) {
00808                 if (length > 0) {
00809                     JAVA_ERROR(context, "Bad deprecated size");
00810                 }
00811                 fb->deprecated = true;
00812             } else if (strcmp(attr_name, "Synthetic") == 0) {
00813                 if (length > 0) {
00814                     JAVA_ERROR(context, "Bad synthetic attribute size");
00815                 }
00816                 fb->synthetic = true;
00817             } else {
00818                 getNbytes(context, length, NULL);
00819             }
00820         }
00821         /*
00822         if (fb->access & ACC_STATIC) {
00823             InitializeStaticVar(fb, context);
00824         }
00825         */
00826     }
00827 
00828     if ((ucb->methods_count = get2bytes(context)) > 0)
00829         ucb->methods = (struct methodblock *)
00830           allocNBytes(context, ucb->methods_count * sizeof(struct methodblock));
00831 
00832     for (i = cbMethodsCount(cb), mb = cbMethods(cb); --i >= 0; mb++) {
00833         bool have_exceptions = false;
00834         fieldclass(&mb->fb) = cb;
00835         mb->fb.access = get2bytes(context) & ACC_WRITTEN_FLAGS;
00836         mb->fb.name = getAsciz(context, false);
00837         mb->fb.signature = getAsciz(context, false);
00838 
00839         if (strcmp(mb->fb.name, "<clinit>") == 0 &&
00840             strcmp(mb->fb.signature, "()V") == 0)
00841             context->in_clinit = true;
00842 
00843         mb->args_size = Signature2ArgsSize(mb->fb.signature)
00844                     + ((mb->fb.access & ACC_STATIC) ? 0 : 1);
00845         if (mb->args_size > 255)
00846             JAVA_ERROR(context, "Too many arguments");
00847 
00848         attribute_count = get2bytes(context);
00849         for (j = 0; j < attribute_count; j++) {
00850             char *attr_name = getAsciz(context, false);
00851             if (strcmp(attr_name, "Code") == 0) {
00852                 if  ((mb->fb.access & (ACC_NATIVE | ACC_ABSTRACT))==0) {
00853                     ReadInCode(context, mb);
00854                 } else {
00855                     JAVA_ERROR(context, "Abstract and native methods cannot "
00856                                         "have code");
00857                 }
00858             } else if (strcmp(attr_name, "Exceptions") == 0) {
00859                 if (have_exceptions) {
00860                     JAVA_ERROR(context, "Multiple Exceptions attribute");
00861                 } else {
00862                     ReadExceptions(context, mb);
00863                     have_exceptions = true;
00864                 }
00865             } else {
00866                 int length = get4bytes(context);
00867                 if (strcmp(attr_name, "Deprecated") == 0) {
00868                     if (length > 0) {
00869                         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Bad deprecated size");
00870                     }
00871                     mb->fb.deprecated = true;
00872                 } else if (strcmp(attr_name, "Synthetic") == 0) {
00873                     if (length > 0) {
00874                         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Bad synthetic attribute size");
00875                     }
00876                     mb->fb.synthetic = true;
00877                 } else {
00878                     getNbytes(context, length, NULL);
00879                 }
00880             }
00881         }
00882         context->in_clinit = false;
00883     }
00884 
00885     /* See if there are class attributes */
00886     attribute_count = get2bytes(context);
00887     have_inner_classes = false;
00888     for (j = 0; j < attribute_count; j++) {
00889         char *attr_name = getAsciz(context, false);
00890         int length = get4bytes(context);
00891         if (strcmp(attr_name, "SourceFile") == 0) {
00892             if (length != 2) {
00893                 JAVA_ERROR(context, "Wrong size for VALUE attribute");
00894             }
00895             ucb->source_name = getAsciz(context, false);
00896         } else if (strcmp(attr_name, "AbsoluteSourcePath") == 0) {
00897             if (length == 2) {
00898                 ucb->absolute_source_name = getAsciz(context, false);
00899             } else
00900             getNbytes(context, length, NULL);
00901         } else if (strcmp(attr_name, "TimeStamp") == 0) {
00902             if (length == 8) {
00903                 ucb->hasTimeStamp = true;
00904                 ucb->timestamp.high = get4bytes(context);
00905                 ucb->timestamp.low = get4bytes(context);
00906             } else {
00907                 getNbytes(context, length, NULL);
00908             }
00909         } else if (strcmp(attr_name, "Deprecated") == 0) {
00910             if (length > 0) {
00911                 JAVA_ERROR(context, "Bad deprecated size");
00912             }
00913             ucb->deprecated = true;
00914         } else if (strcmp(attr_name, "Synthetic") == 0) {
00915             if (length > 0) {
00916                 JAVA_ERROR(context, "Bad synthetic attribute size");
00917             }
00918             ucb->synthetic = true;
00919         } else if (strcmp(attr_name, "InnerClasses") == 0) {
00920             int count = get2bytes(context);
00921             struct innerClasses *thisInnerClass = (struct innerClasses *)
00922                  allocNBytes(context, count * sizeof(struct innerClasses));
00923             struct innerClasses *lastInnerClass = thisInnerClass + count;
00924 
00925             if (count * 8 + 2 != length) {
00926                 JAVA_ERROR(context, "Bad length of InnerClasses attribute");
00927             }
00928 
00929             if (have_inner_classes) {
00930                 JAVA_ERROR(context, "Duplicate InnerClasses attribute");
00931             }
00932             have_inner_classes = false;
00933 
00934             ucb->inner_classes_count = count;
00935             ucb->inner_classes = thisInnerClass;
00936 
00937             for ( ; thisInnerClass < lastInnerClass; thisInnerClass++) {
00938                 thisInnerClass->inner_class = get2bytes(context);
00939                 thisInnerClass->outer_class = get2bytes(context);
00940                 thisInnerClass->inner_name = getAsciz(context, true);
00941                 thisInnerClass->access = get2bytes(context);
00942                 if (thisInnerClass->inner_class != 0) {
00943                     /* Make sure that inner_class really belongs to a CLASS */
00944                     getAscizFromClass(context, thisInnerClass->inner_class);
00945                 }
00946                 if (thisInnerClass->outer_class != 0) {
00947                     /* Make sure that outer_class really belongs to a CLASS */
00948                     getAscizFromClass(context, thisInnerClass->outer_class);
00949                 }
00950             }
00951         } else {
00952             getNbytes(context, length, NULL);
00953         }
00954     }
00955 
00956     /* Make sure that we have read all of the bytes */
00957     if (context->end_ptr != context->ptr) {
00958         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Junk at end of class file");
00959     }
00960 
00961 }
00962 
00963 /*=========================================================================
00964  * FUNCTION:      createFakeArrayClass
00965  * OVERVIEW:      Invoked by Locked_FindArrayClassFromClass() for creating
00966  *                a fake array class that has the specified fields.
00967  *
00968  * INTERFACE:
00969  *   parameters:  char *: name
00970  *                int : base type
00971  *                int : depth  (array dimension)
00972  *                ClassClass * : base type if T_CLASS
00973  *                struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *: class loader
00974  *
00975  *   returns:     ClassClass *
00976  *=======================================================================*/
00977 ClassClass *
00978 createFakeArrayClass(char *name,   /* name */
00979              int base_type,        /* base_type */
00980              int depth,            /* array dimension */
00981              ClassClass *inner_cb, /* base type if T_CLASS */
00982              struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *loader)
00983 {
00984     ClassClass *cb = allocClassClass();
00985     Classjava_lang_Class *ucb = unhand(cb);
00986     cp_item_type *constant_pool =
00987     sysCalloc(CONSTANT_POOL_ARRAY_LENGTH,
00988           (sizeof(cp_item_type) + sizeof(unsigned char)));
00989     unsigned char *type_table = (unsigned char *)
00990         (constant_pool + CONSTANT_POOL_ARRAY_LENGTH);
00991     sysAssert(name[0] == SIGNATURE_ARRAY);
00992     ucb->major_version = JAVA_VERSION;
00993     ucb->minor_version = JAVA_MINOR_VERSION;
00994     ucb->name = strdup(name);
00995     ucb->super_name = JAVAPKG "Object";
00996     ucb->constantpool = constant_pool;
00997     ucb->constantpool_count = CONSTANT_POOL_ARRAY_LENGTH;
00998     ucb->loader = loader;
00999 
01000     constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type = type_table;
01001     constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_ARRAY_DEPTH_INDEX].i = depth;
01002     constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_ARRAY_TYPE_INDEX].i = base_type;
01003     type_table[CONSTANT_POOL_ARRAY_DEPTH_INDEX] =
01004     CONSTANT_Integer | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED;
01005     type_table[CONSTANT_POOL_ARRAY_TYPE_INDEX] =
01006     CONSTANT_Integer | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED;
01007 
01008     if (base_type == T_CLASS) {
01009         /* Initialize the appropriate fields of the constant pool */
01010         constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_ARRAY_CLASS_INDEX].clazz = inner_cb;
01011         type_table[CONSTANT_POOL_ARRAY_CLASS_INDEX] =
01012                       CONSTANT_Class | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED;
01013         /* The class is public iff its base class is public */
01014         ucb->access = ACC_FINAL | ACC_ABSTRACT |
01015                       (cbAccess(inner_cb) & ACC_PUBLIC);
01016     } else {
01017         /* Set the class field to something innocuous */
01018         type_table[CONSTANT_POOL_ARRAY_CLASS_INDEX] =
01019                       CONSTANT_Integer | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED;
01020         ucb->access = ACC_FINAL | ACC_ABSTRACT | ACC_PUBLIC;
01021     }
01022     AddBinClass(cb);
01023     return cb;
01024 }
01025 
01026 /*=========================================================================
01027  * FUNCTION:      createPrimitiveClass
01028  * OVERVIEW:      Creates a class block to represent a primitive type.
01029  *                NOTE: this is not added to the built-in class table, so
01030  *                      it should only be called once per primitive type.
01031  *                      See FindPrimitiveClass().
01032  *
01033  * INTERFACE:
01034  *   parameters:  char *: name
01035  *                char  : sig
01036  *                unsigned char: typecode
01037  *                unsigned char: slotsize
01038  *                unsigned char: elementsize
01039  *
01040  *   returns:     ClassClass *
01041  *=======================================================================*/
01042 ClassClass *
01043 createPrimitiveClass(char *name, char sig, unsigned char typecode,
01044     unsigned char slotsize, unsigned char elementsize)
01045 {
01046     ClassClass *cb = allocClassClass();
01047     Classjava_lang_Class *ucb = unhand(cb);
01048 
01049     ucb->major_version = JAVA_VERSION;
01050     ucb->minor_version = JAVA_MINOR_VERSION;
01051     ucb->name = strdup(name);
01052     ucb->super_name = JAVAPKG "Object";
01053     ucb->constantpool = NULL;
01054     ucb->constantpool_count = 0;
01055     ucb->loader = NULL;
01056     ucb->access = ACC_FINAL | ACC_ABSTRACT | ACC_PUBLIC;
01057 
01058     CCSet(cb, Primitive);
01059     cbTypeSig(cb) = sig;
01060     cbTypeCode(cb) = typecode;
01061     cbSlotSize(cb) = slotsize;
01062     cbElementSize(cb) = elementsize;
01063     MakeClassSticky(cb);
01064 
01065     return cb;
01066 }
01067 
01068 /*=========================================================================
01069  * FUNCTION:      LoadConstantPool
01070  * OVERVIEW:      Loads the constant pool given a pointer to the internal
01071  *                class file.
01072  *
01073  * INTERFACE:
01074  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: ptr
01075  *
01076  *   returns:     nothing
01077  *=======================================================================*/
01078 static void LoadConstantPool(CICcontext *context)
01079 {
01080     ClassClass *cb = context->cb;
01081     int nconstants = get2bytes(context);
01082     cp_item_type *constant_pool;
01083     unsigned char *type_table;
01084     int i;
01085     Java8 t1;
01086 
01087     if (nconstants > 16384) {
01088         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Preverifier only "
01089                    "handles constant pool size up to 16K");
01090     }
01091 
01092     t1.x[0] = 0; /* shut off warning */
01093     if (nconstants < CONSTANT_POOL_UNUSED_INDEX) {
01094         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Illegal constant pool size");
01095     }
01096 
01097     constant_pool = (cp_item_type *)
01098         allocNBytes(context, nconstants * sizeof(cp_item_type));
01099     type_table = allocNBytes(context, nconstants * sizeof(char));
01100 
01101     for (i = CONSTANT_POOL_UNUSED_INDEX; i < nconstants; i++) {
01102         int type = get1byte(context);
01103         CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_PUT(type_table, i, type);
01104         switch (type) {
01105             case CONSTANT_Utf8: {
01106                 int length = get2bytes(context);
01107                 char *result = allocNBytes(context, length + 1);
01108                 getNbytes(context, length, result);
01109                 result[length] = '\0';
01110                 constant_pool[i].cp = result;
01111                 CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_SET_RESOLVED(type_table, i);
01112                 verifyUTF8String(context, result, length);
01113                 break;
01114             }
01115 
01116             case CONSTANT_Class:
01117             case CONSTANT_String:
01118                 constant_pool[i].i = get2bytes(context);
01119                 break;
01120 
01121             case CONSTANT_Fieldref:
01122             case CONSTANT_Methodref:
01123             case CONSTANT_InterfaceMethodref:
01124             case CONSTANT_NameAndType:
01125                 constant_pool[i].i = get4bytes(context);
01126                 break;
01127 
01128             case CONSTANT_Float:
01129                 if (no_floating_point) {
01130                     panic("floating-point constants should not appear");
01131                 } else {
01132                    constant_pool[i].i = get4bytes(context);
01133                    CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_SET_RESOLVED(type_table, i);
01134                    break;
01135                 }
01136 
01137             case CONSTANT_Integer:
01138                 constant_pool[i].i = get4bytes(context);
01139                 CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_SET_RESOLVED(type_table, i);
01140                 break;
01141 
01142             case CONSTANT_Double:
01143             case CONSTANT_Long: {
01144                 if (type == CONSTANT_Double) {
01145                    if (no_floating_point) {
01146                       panic("floating-point constants should not appear");
01147                    }
01148                 }
01149                 /* We ignore endian problems, and just load the two
01150                  * values.  The verifier never actually cares what
01151                  * the actual value is.
01152                  */
01153                 constant_pool[i].i = get4bytes(context);
01154                 CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_SET_RESOLVED(type_table, i);
01155                 i++;
01156                 if (i >= nconstants) {
01157                     JAVA_ERROR(context, "illegal constant pool entry");
01158                 }
01159                 /* Indicate that the next object in the constant pool cannot
01160                  * be accessed independently.
01161                  */
01162                 constant_pool[i].i = get4bytes(context);
01163                 CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_PUT(type_table, i, 0);
01164                 CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_SET_RESOLVED(type_table, i);
01165                 break;
01166             }
01167 
01168             default:
01169                 JAVA_ERROR(context, "Illegal constant pool type");
01170         }
01171     }
01172     /* It is important to only set these after everything is setup,
01173        so that the GC sees a consistent state.*/
01174     cbConstantPool(cb) = constant_pool;
01175     cbConstantPoolCount(cb) = nconstants;
01176     constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type = type_table;
01177 }
01178 
01179 static void
01180 verifyUTF8String(CICcontext *context, char* bytes, unsigned short length)
01181 {
01182     unsigned int i;
01183     for (i=0; i<length; i++) {
01184         unsigned int c = ((unsigned char *)bytes)[i];
01185         if (c == 0) /* no embedded zeros */
01186             goto failed;
01187         if (c < 128)
01188             continue;
01189         switch (c >> 4) {
01190         default:
01191             break;
01192 
01193         case 0x8: case 0x9: case 0xA: case 0xB: case 0xF:
01194             goto failed;
01195 
01196         case 0xC: case 0xD:
01197             /* 110xxxxx  10xxxxxx */
01198             i++;
01199             if (i >= length)
01200                 goto failed;
01201             if ((bytes[i] & 0xC0) == 0x80)
01202                 break;
01203             goto failed;
01204 
01205         case 0xE:
01206             /* 1110xxxx 10xxxxxx 10xxxxxx */
01207             i += 2;
01208             if (i >= length)
01209                 goto failed;
01210             if (((bytes[i-1] & 0xC0) == 0x80) &&
01211                 ((bytes[i] & 0xC0) == 0x80))
01212                 break;
01213             goto failed;
01214         } /* end of switch */
01215     }
01216     return;
01217 
01218  failed:
01219     JAVA_ERROR(context, "Bad utf string");
01220 }
01221 
01222 /*=========================================================================
01223  * FUNCTION:      ReadInCode
01224  * OVERVIEW:      Reads the code attributes given a pointer to the internal
01225  *                class file and a pointer to the method block structure.
01226  *                This includes line number and local variable tables.
01227  *
01228  * INTERFACE:
01229  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: ptr
01230  *                struct methodblock *: mb
01231  *
01232  *   returns:     nothing
01233  *=======================================================================*/
01234 static void ReadInCode(CICcontext *context, struct methodblock *mb)
01235 {
01236     int attribute_length = get4bytes(context);
01237     unsigned char *end_ptr = context->ptr + attribute_length;
01238     int attribute_count;
01239     int code_length;
01240     int i;
01241 
01242     if (cbMinorVersion(context->cb) == JAVA_VERSION &&
01243         cbMinorVersion(context->cb) <= 2) {
01244         mb->maxstack = get1byte(context);
01245         mb->nlocals = get1byte(context);
01246         code_length = mb->code_length = get2bytes(context);
01247     } else {
01248         mb->maxstack = get2bytes(context);
01249         mb->nlocals = get2bytes(context);
01250         code_length = mb->code_length = get4bytes(context);
01251     }
01252     if (mb->nlocals < mb->args_size)
01253         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Arguments can't fit into locals");
01254 
01255     if (code_length > 65535) {
01256         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Byte code size exceeds 65535 bytes");
01257     }
01258 
01259     mb->code = allocNBytes(context, code_length);
01260 
01261     getNbytes(context, code_length, (char *)mb->code);
01262     if ((mb->exception_table_length = get2bytes(context)) > 0) {
01263         unsigned exception_table_size = mb->exception_table_length
01264                                       * sizeof(struct CatchFrame);
01265         mb->exception_table = allocNBytes(context, exception_table_size);
01266         for (i = 0; i < (int)mb->exception_table_length; i++) {
01267             mb->exception_table[i].start_pc = get2bytes(context);
01268             mb->exception_table[i].end_pc = get2bytes(context);
01269             mb->exception_table[i].handler_pc = get2bytes(context);
01270             mb->exception_table[i].catchType = get2bytes(context);
01271             mb->exception_table[i].compiled_CatchFrame = NULL;
01272         }
01273     }
01274     attribute_count = get2bytes(context);
01275     for (i = 0; i < attribute_count; i++) {
01276         char *name = getAsciz(context, false);
01277         if (strcmp(name, "LineNumberTable") == 0) {
01278             ReadLineTable(context, mb);
01279         } else if (strcmp(name, "LocalVariableTable") == 0) {
01280             ReadLocalVars(context, mb);
01281         } else {
01282             int length = get4bytes(context);
01283             getNbytes(context, length, NULL);
01284         }
01285     }
01286     if (context->ptr != end_ptr)
01287         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Code segment was wrong length");
01288 }
01289 
01290 /*=========================================================================
01291  * FUNCTION:      ReadLineTable
01292  * OVERVIEW:      Reads the line number table given a pointer to the internal
01293  *                class file and a pointer to the method block structure.
01294  *
01295  * INTERFACE:
01296  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: ptr
01297  *                struct methodblock *: mb
01298  *
01299  *   returns:     nothing
01300  *=======================================================================*/
01301 static void ReadLineTable(CICcontext *context, struct methodblock *mb)
01302 {
01303     int attribute_length = get4bytes(context);
01304     unsigned char *end_ptr = context->ptr  + attribute_length;
01305     int i;
01306     if ((mb->line_number_table_length = get2bytes(context)) > 0) {
01307         struct lineno *ln =
01308                allocNBytes(context, mb->line_number_table_length *
01309                            sizeof(struct lineno));
01310         mb->line_number_table = ln;
01311         for (i = mb->line_number_table_length; --i >= 0; ln++) {
01312             ln->pc = get2bytes(context);
01313             ln->line_number = get2bytes(context);
01314         }
01315     }
01316     if (context->ptr != end_ptr)
01317         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Line number table was wrong length?");
01318 }
01319 
01320 /*=========================================================================
01321  * FUNCTION:      ReadLocalVars
01322  * OVERVIEW:      Reads the localvar table given a pointer to the internal
01323  *                class file and a pointer to the method block structure.
01324  *
01325  * INTERFACE:
01326  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: ptr
01327  *                struct methodblock *: mb
01328  *
01329  *   returns:     nothing
01330  *=======================================================================*/
01331 static void ReadLocalVars(CICcontext *context, struct methodblock *mb)
01332 {
01333     int attribute_length = get4bytes(context);
01334     unsigned char *end_ptr = context->ptr  + attribute_length;
01335     int i;
01336     if ((mb->localvar_table_length = get2bytes(context)) > 0) {
01337         struct localvar *lv =
01338                allocNBytes(context, mb->localvar_table_length *
01339                            sizeof(struct localvar));
01340         mb->localvar_table = lv;
01341         for (i = mb->localvar_table_length; --i >= 0; lv++) {
01342             lv->pc0 = get2bytes(context);
01343             lv->length = get2bytes(context);
01344             lv->name = getAsciz(context, false);
01345             lv->signature = getAsciz(context, false);
01346             lv->slot = get2bytes(context);
01347         }
01348     }
01349     if (context->ptr != end_ptr)
01350         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Local variables table was wrong length?");
01351 }
01352 
01353 /*=========================================================================
01354  * FUNCTION:      ReadExceptions
01355  * OVERVIEW:      Reads the Exception attribute given a pointer to the internal
01356  *                class file and a pointer to the method block structure.
01357  *
01358  * INTERFACE:
01359  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: ptr
01360  *                struct methodblock *: mb
01361  *
01362  *   returns:     nothing
01363  *=======================================================================*/
01364 static void
01365 ReadExceptions(CICcontext *context, struct methodblock *mb)
01366 {
01367     int attribute_length = get4bytes(context);
01368     unsigned char *end_ptr = context->ptr + attribute_length;
01369     unsigned short nexceptions = get2bytes(context);
01370 
01371     if ((mb->nexceptions = nexceptions) > 0) {
01372         unsigned short *ep, *exceptions =
01373             allocNBytes(context, nexceptions * sizeof (unsigned short));
01374         mb->exceptions = ep = exceptions;
01375         while (nexceptions-- > 0) {
01376             *ep++ = get2bytes(context);
01377         }
01378     }
01379     if (context->ptr != end_ptr)
01380         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Exceptions attribute has wrong length");
01381 }
01382 
01383 /*=========================================================================
01384  * FUNCTION:      Signature2ArgsSize
01385  * OVERVIEW:      Returns the size of arguments given a pointer to a method
01386  *                signature.
01387  *
01388  * INTERFACE:
01389  *   parameters:  char*: method_signature
01390  *
01391  *   returns:     unsigned
01392  *=======================================================================*/
01393 unsigned Signature2ArgsSize(char *method_signature)
01394 {
01395     char *p;
01396     int args_size = 0;
01397     for (p = method_signature; *p != SIGNATURE_ENDFUNC; p++) {
01398         switch (*p) {
01399             case SIGNATURE_FLOAT:
01400                 if (no_floating_point) {
01401                     panic("floating-point arguments should not appear");
01402                 } else {
01403                     args_size += 1;
01404                     break;
01405                 }
01406 
01407             case SIGNATURE_BOOLEAN:
01408             case SIGNATURE_BYTE:
01409             case SIGNATURE_CHAR:
01410             case SIGNATURE_SHORT:
01411             case SIGNATURE_INT:
01412                 args_size += 1;
01413                 break;
01414 
01415             case SIGNATURE_CLASS:
01416                 args_size += 1;
01417                 while (*p != SIGNATURE_ENDCLASS) p++;
01418                 break;
01419 
01420             case SIGNATURE_ARRAY:
01421                 args_size += 1;
01422                 while ((*p == SIGNATURE_ARRAY)) p++;
01423                     /* If an array of classes, skip over class name, too. */
01424                     if (*p == SIGNATURE_CLASS) {
01425                         while (*p != SIGNATURE_ENDCLASS)
01426                             p++;
01427                     }
01428                 break;
01429 
01430             case SIGNATURE_DOUBLE:
01431                 if (no_floating_point) {
01432                     panic("floating-point arguments should not appear");
01433                 } else {
01434                     args_size += 2;
01435                     break;
01436                 }
01437 
01438             case SIGNATURE_LONG:
01439                 args_size += 2;
01440                 break;
01441 
01442             case SIGNATURE_FUNC:  /* ignore initial (, if given */
01443                 break;
01444 
01445             default:              /* Indicates an error. */
01446                 return 0;
01447         }
01448     }
01449     return args_size;
01450 }
01451 
01452 /*=========================================================================
01453  * FUNCTION:      free_clinit_memory
01454  * OVERVIEW:      Frees clinit memory.
01455  *
01456  * INTERFACE:
01457  *   parameters:  struct methodblock *: mb
01458  *
01459  *   returns:     nothing
01460  *=======================================================================*/
01461 void free_clinit_memory(struct methodblock *mb)
01462 {
01463     /* This function is somewhat a wokraround.
01464      * sysFree may be called on the wrong memory block if
01465      * the exception attribute comes before the code attribute.
01466      */
01467     /* If there is no exceptions attribute, or if both have already
01468      * been freed.
01469      */
01470     if (mb->exceptions == NULL) {
01471         if (mb->code) {
01472             sysFree(mb->code);
01473             mb->code = NULL;
01474         }
01475         return;
01476     }
01477 
01478     /* If both attributes exist, free the one at the lower address */
01479     if ((char *)mb->code < (char *)mb->exceptions)
01480         sysFree(mb->code);
01481     else
01482         sysFree(mb->exceptions);
01483 
01484     mb->code = NULL;
01485     mb->exceptions = NULL;
01486 }
01487 
01488 /*=========================================================================
01489  * FUNCTION:      FreeClass
01490  * OVERVIEW:      Frees class.
01491  *
01492  * INTERFACE:
01493  *   parameters:  ClassClass *: cb
01494  *
01495  *   returns:     nothing
01496  *=======================================================================*/
01497 void FreeClass(ClassClass *cb)
01498 {
01499     int i;
01500     struct methodblock *mb;
01501 
01502     for (i = cbMethodsCount(cb), mb = cbMethods(cb); --i >= 0; mb++) {
01503         if (strcmp(mb->fb.name, "<clinit>") == 0 &&
01504             strcmp(mb->fb.signature, "()V") == 0 &&
01505             mb->code_length /* not external */ )
01506             free_clinit_memory(mb);
01507     }
01508 
01509     sysFree(cbConstantPool(cb));
01510 
01511     sysFree(cbMethodTableMem(cb));
01512     sysFree(cbSlotTable(cb));
01513 
01514     /* Interface method tables can be shared between child and
01515      * super classes.
01516      */
01517     if (cbImplementsCount(cb) != 0 || cbIsInterface(cb))
01518         sysFree(cbIntfMethodTable(cb));
01519 }
01520 
01521 /*=========================================================================
01522  * FUNCTION:      get1byte
01523  * OVERVIEW:      Gets one byte from the class file.
01524  *
01525  * INTERFACE:
01526  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: context
01527  *
01528  *   returns:     value read or 0 if an error occurred.
01529  *=======================================================================*/
01530 static uint8_t get1byte(CICcontext *context)
01531 {
01532     unsigned char *ptr = context->ptr;
01533     if (context->end_ptr - ptr < 1) {
01534         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Truncated class file");
01535         return 0;
01536     } else {
01537         uint8_t *ptr = context->ptr;
01538         uint8_t value = ptr[0];
01539         (context->ptr) += 1;
01540         return value;
01541     }
01542 }
01543 
01544 /*=========================================================================
01545  * FUNCTION:      get2bytes
01546  * OVERVIEW:      Gets two bytes from the class file.
01547  *
01548  * INTERFACE:
01549  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: context
01550  *
01551  *   returns:     value read or 0 if an error occurred.
01552  *=======================================================================*/
01553 static uint16_t get2bytes(CICcontext *context)
01554 {
01555     unsigned char *ptr = context->ptr;
01556     if (context->end_ptr - ptr < 2) {
01557         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Truncated class file");
01558         return 0;
01559     } else {
01560         uint16_t value = (ptr[0] << 8) + ptr[1];
01561         (context->ptr) += 2;
01562         return value;
01563     }
01564 }
01565 
01566 /*=========================================================================
01567  * FUNCTION:      get4bytes
01568  * OVERVIEW:      Gets four bytes from the class file.
01569  *
01570  * INTERFACE:
01571  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: context
01572  *
01573  *   returns:     value read or 0 if an error occurred.
01574  *=======================================================================*/
01575 static uint32_t get4bytes(CICcontext *context)
01576 {
01577     unsigned char *ptr = context->ptr;
01578     if (context->end_ptr - ptr < 4) {
01579         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Truncated class file");
01580         return 0;
01581     } else {
01582         uint32_t value = (ptr[0] << 24) + (ptr[1] << 16) +
01583                                  (ptr[2] << 8) + ptr[3];
01584         (context->ptr) += 4;
01585         return value;
01586     }
01587 }
01588 
01589 /*=========================================================================
01590  * FUNCTION:      getNbytes
01591  * OVERVIEW:      Gets N bytes from the class file specified by the count
01592  *                parameter. If buffer is not null, it will also copy the
01593  *                count number of bytes read into the buffer as well.
01594  *                Note that this function seems to be always invoked with
01595  *                a NULL argument for the buffer, except when it loads the
01596  *                UTF8 entry from the constant pool and when the code
01597  *                attribute is loaded.
01598  *
01599  * INTERFACE:
01600  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: context
01601  *                int: count
01602  *                char *: buffer
01603  *
01604  *   returns:     nothing
01605  *=======================================================================*/
01606 static void getNbytes(CICcontext *context, int count, char *buffer)
01607 {
01608     unsigned char *ptr = context->ptr;
01609     if (context->end_ptr - ptr < count)
01610         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Truncated class file");
01611     if (buffer != NULL)
01612         memcpy(buffer, ptr, count);
01613     (context->ptr) += count;
01614 }
01615 
01616 /*=========================================================================
01617  * FUNCTION:      getAsciz
01618  * OVERVIEW:      Reads the next two bytes and uses this value to look up for
01619  *                the corresponding constant pool entry.
01620  *                Returns null if the value is 0 and zeroOkay flag is set.
01621  *
01622  * INTERFACE:
01623  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: context
01624  *                bool_t : zeroOkay
01625  *
01626  *   returns:     char * or NULL
01627  *=======================================================================*/
01628 static char *getAsciz(CICcontext *context, bool zeroOkay)
01629 {
01630     ClassClass *cb = context->cb;
01631     union cp_item_type *constant_pool = cbConstantPool(cb);
01632     int nconstants = cbConstantPoolCount(cb);
01633     unsigned char *type_table =
01634         constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
01635 
01636     int value = get2bytes(context);
01637     if (value == 0 && zeroOkay) {
01638         return NULL;
01639     } else if ((value == 0) || (value >= nconstants) ||
01640         type_table[value] != (CONSTANT_Utf8 | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED))
01641         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Illegal constant pool index");
01642     return constant_pool[value].cp;
01643 }
01644 
01645 /*=========================================================================
01646  * FUNCTION:      getAscizFromClass
01647  * OVERVIEW:      Given the constant pool index, returns the name of the class
01648  *                which corresponds to this constant pool entry.
01649  *
01650  * INTERFACE:
01651  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: context
01652  *                int: value
01653  *
01654  *   returns:     char * or NULL
01655  *=======================================================================*/
01656 static char *getAscizFromClass(CICcontext *context, int value)
01657 {
01658     ClassClass *cb = context->cb;
01659     union cp_item_type *constant_pool = cbConstantPool(cb);
01660     int nconstants = cbConstantPoolCount(cb);
01661     unsigned char *type_table =
01662     constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
01663     if ((value > 0) && (value < nconstants)) {
01664         if (type_table[value] == CONSTANT_Class) {
01665             value = constant_pool[value].i;
01666             if ((value <= 0) || (value >= nconstants) ||
01667                 (type_table[value] != (CONSTANT_Utf8 |
01668                        CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED)))
01669                 JAVA_ERROR(context, "Illegal constant pool index");
01670             return constant_pool[value].cp;
01671         } else if (type_table[value] == (CONSTANT_Class |
01672                                  CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED)) {
01673             ClassClass *cb = constant_pool[value].clazz;
01674             return cbName(cb);
01675         } else {
01676             JAVA_ERROR(context, "Illegal constant pool index");
01677         }
01678     } else {
01679         JAVA_ERROR(context, "Illegal constant pool index");
01680     }
01681     return NULL; /* not reached */
01682 }
01683 
01684 /* In order to avoid possible alignment errors, round up all sizes to
01685  * multiples of eight.
01686  */
01687 #define ROUNDUP_SIZE(s) while ((s) % 8 != 0) (s)++
01688 
01689 /*=========================================================================
01690  * FUNCTION:      allocNBytes
01691  * OVERVIEW:      Memory allocation function for internal class file
01692  *                structures.
01693  *                It calculates the number of allocations needed for the
01694  *                two passes, and the allocations required for the clinit
01695  *                method.
01696  *
01697  * INTERFACE:
01698  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: context
01699  *                int : size
01700  *
01701  *   returns:     void *
01702  *=======================================================================*/
01703 static void *allocNBytes(CICcontext *context, int size)
01704 {
01705     void *result;
01706     if (context->pass == 1) {
01707         /* The first pass
01708          * A more sophisticated scheme could reduce the number of mallocs.
01709          */
01710         CICmallocs *mallocs =
01711            (CICmallocs *)sysCalloc(1, sizeof(CICmallocs) + size);
01712         if (mallocs == 0)
01713             JAVA_ERROR(context, "out of memory");
01714         result = (void *)(mallocs + 1);
01715         mallocs->next = context->pass1.mallocs;
01716         ROUNDUP_SIZE(size);
01717         if (context->in_clinit)
01718             context->clinit_size += size;
01719         else
01720             context->malloc_size += size;
01721         context->pass1.mallocs = mallocs;
01722     } else {
01723         /* The second pass */
01724 
01725         ROUNDUP_SIZE(size);
01726 
01727 #define ALLOC_BLOCK(ptr,buf,sizelimit) \
01728         result = (ptr);                \
01729         (ptr) += (size);               \
01730         sysAssert((ptr) <= (buf) + (sizelimit))
01731 
01732         if (context->in_clinit) {
01733             /* Make sure that this clinit pointer is not null */
01734             sysAssert(context->pass2.clinit_ptr != NULL);
01735 
01736             ALLOC_BLOCK(context->pass2.clinit_ptr,
01737             context->pass2.clinit_buffer,
01738             context->clinit_size*sizeof(char));
01739 
01740         } else {
01741 
01742             /* Make sure that this malloc pointer is not null */
01743             sysAssert(context->pass2.malloc_ptr != NULL);
01744 
01745             ALLOC_BLOCK(context->pass2.malloc_ptr,
01746             context->pass2.malloc_buffer,
01747             context->malloc_size);
01748         }
01749     }
01750     return result;
01751 }
01752 
01753 /*=========================================================================
01754  * FUNCTION:      freeBuffers
01755  * OVERVIEW:      Frees buffers allocated by allocNBytes().
01756  *
01757  * INTERFACE:
01758  *   parameters:  CICcontext *: context
01759  *
01760  *   returns:     nothing
01761  *=======================================================================*/
01762 static void freeBuffers(CICcontext * context)
01763 {
01764     if (context->pass == 1) {
01765         CICmallocs *mallocs = context->pass1.mallocs;
01766 
01767         while (mallocs) {
01768             CICmallocs *tmp = mallocs;
01769             mallocs = mallocs->next;
01770             if (tmp != NULL) {
01771                 sysFree(tmp);
01772             }
01773         }
01774         context->pass1.mallocs = 0;
01775     } else { /* context->pass = 2 */
01776 
01777 /* Note: this code is here just for historical reasons. Actually, these
01778  * buffers cannot really be freed here since the data in them is still
01779  * pointed to by the class buffer (cb) and we are not yet done loading
01780  * the class. If the class loading fails, FreeClass() will free the method
01781  * blocks and the clinit memory.
01782  */
01783         if (context->pass2.malloc_buffer != NULL) {
01784             sysFree(context->pass2.malloc_buffer);
01785             /* Reset only if buffer was freed */
01786             context->pass2.malloc_buffer = 0;
01787         }
01788 
01789         if (context->pass2.clinit_buffer != NULL) {
01790             sysFree(context->pass2.clinit_buffer);
01791             /* Initialize only if buffer was freed */
01792             context->pass2.clinit_buffer = 0;
01793         }
01794     }
01795 }
01796 
01797 /*=========================================================================
01798  * FUNCTION:      GetClassConstantClassName
01799  * OVERVIEW:      Returns class name corresponding to the constant pool entry
01800  *                for the given cpIndex. This is desirable in cases when we
01801  *                may simply be interested in the name of the class, but may
01802  *                not necessarily want to resolve the class reference if it
01803  *                isn't already.
01804  *
01805  * INTERFACE:
01806  *   parameters:  cp_item_type *: constant pool
01807  *                int : index
01808  *
01809  *   returns:     char *: class name
01810  *=======================================================================*/
01811 char *GetClassConstantClassName(cp_item_type *constant_pool, int index)
01812 {
01813     unsigned char *type_table =
01814         constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
01815     switch(type_table[index]) {
01816         case CONSTANT_Class | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED: {
01817             ClassClass *cb = constant_pool[index].clazz;
01818             return cbName(cb);
01819         }
01820 
01821         case CONSTANT_Class: {
01822             int name_index = constant_pool[index].i;
01823             return constant_pool[name_index].cp;
01824         }
01825 
01826         default:
01827             return (char *)0;
01828     }
01829 }
01830
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:27 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
